article.body_preview:
Dhaka, Jan 31 (EFE).- The International Monetary Fund (IMF) has approved a $4.7 billion loan package for Bangladesh amid a worrying state of the country's economy.

The approval for the 42-month borrowing package came in the IMF Executive Board meeting in Washington on Monday.

Bangladesh would get $3.3 billion under the Extended Credit Facility and the Extended Fund Facility arrangements.

The IMF Executive Board also approved $1.4 billion under the newly created Resilience and Sustainability F

llm_connections.evidence_quote: It has led to a sharp widening of Bangladesh's current account deficit, depreciation of the taka, and a decline in foreign exchange reserves.
llm_connections.scope_justification: The article explicitly states a country-level 'depreciation of the taka', which is an exchange-rate movement matching the currency_log_return definition of national exchange-rate volatility.
